# UC-10.15.5 · Risk Score Calibration with Supervised ML (MLTK)

## Description

Risk-Based Alerting assigns scores heuristically, but not all risk events are equal. Training a supervised model on analyst-confirmed true/false positives re-weights risk contributions so the aggregate score better predicts real incidents — reducing alert fatigue and focusing SOC attention on the highest-fidelity signals.

## Value

Risk-Based Alerting assigns scores heuristically, but not all risk events are equal. Training a supervised model on analyst-confirmed true/false positives re-weights risk contributions so the aggregate score better predicts real incidents — reducing alert fatigue and focusing SOC attention on the highest-fidelity signals.

## Implementation

Export 90+ days of closed notables with analyst dispositions (true positive, false positive) from ES. Join to the Risk datamodel to build feature vectors per risk object: total risk score, contributing source count, risk event types, time spread, and asset criticality. Train a RandomForestClassifier or GradientBoostedTrees model monthly. Use the model to re-score incoming risk aggregations, replacing or supplementing the raw sum. Publish re-calibrated scores as a lookup that ES correlation searches reference. Track model precision/recall in a dashboard to measure SOC efficiency gains over time.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Machine Learning Toolkit (MLTK), Splunk Enterprise Security (ES).
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=risk`, `index=notable` (with analyst disposition), ES identity and asset lookups.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Export 90+ days of closed notables with analyst dispositions (true positive, false positive) from ES. Join to the Risk datamodel to build feature vectors per risk object: total risk score, contributing source count, risk event types, time spread, and asset criticality. Train a RandomForestClassifier or GradientBoostedTrees model monthly. Use the model to re-score incoming risk aggregations, replacing or supplementing the raw sum. Publish re-calibrated scores as a lookup that ES correlation searc…

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
| tstats `security_content_summariesonly` count sum(All_Risk.calculated_risk_score) as total_risk
    FROM datamodel=Risk BY All_Risk.risk_object, All_Risk.risk_object_type, All_Risk.source
| `drop_dm_object_name(All_Risk)`
| join type=left risk_object
    [search index=notable status IN ("Closed - True Positive","Closed - False Positive")
    | eval label=if(status="Closed - True Positive", 1, 0)
    | stats max(label) as is_true_positive by risk_object]
| where isnotnull(is_true_positive)
| fit RandomForestClassifier is_true_positive from total_risk, count into risk_calibration_model
| table risk_object, total_risk, count, predicted(is_true_positive), probability
| where 'predicted(is_true_positive)' = 1
| sort -probability
```

Understanding this SPL

**Risk Score Calibration with Supervised ML (MLTK)** — Risk-Based Alerting assigns scores heuristically, but not all risk events are equal. Training a supervised model on analyst-confirmed true/false positives re-weights risk contributions so the aggregate score better predicts real incidents — reducing alert fatigue and focusing SOC attention on the highest-fidelity signals.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=risk`, `index=notable` (with analyst disposition), ES identity and asset lookups. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Machine Learning Toolkit (MLTK), Splunk Enterprise Security (ES). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

**Detection type** for this use case: risk_object — interpret thresholds and fields in that context.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Uses `tstats` against accelerated summaries for data model `Risk` — enable acceleration for that model.
• Invokes macro `drop_dm_object_name(All_Risk)` — in Search, use the UI or expand to inspect the underlying SPL.
• Joins to a subsearch with `join` — set `max=` to match cardinality and avoid silent truncation.
• Filters the current rows with `where isnotnull(is_true_positive)` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• Pipeline stage (see **Risk Score Calibration with Supervised ML (MLTK)**): fit RandomForestClassifier is_true_positive from total_risk, count into risk_calibration_model
• Pipeline stage (see **Risk Score Calibration with Supervised ML (MLTK)**): table risk_object, total_risk, count, predicted(is_true_positive), probability
• Filters the current rows with `where 'predicted(is_true_positive)' = 1` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.

Enable Data Model Acceleration (and metric indexes for `mstats`) for the models or datasets referenced above; otherwise `tstats`/`mstats` may return no results from summaries.


Step 3 — Validate
Compare model scores to **analyst dispositions in Incident Review** and the training export you used, and re-run a small batch through **the MLTK experiment** to verify lift has not regressed after retraining.



Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: ROC curve (model performance), Table (recalibrated risk objects), Bar chart (feature importance from model).


## SPL

```spl
| tstats `security_content_summariesonly` count sum(All_Risk.calculated_risk_score) as total_risk
    FROM datamodel=Risk BY All_Risk.risk_object, All_Risk.risk_object_type, All_Risk.source
| `drop_dm_object_name(All_Risk)`
| join type=left risk_object
    [search index=notable status IN ("Closed - True Positive","Closed - False Positive")
    | eval label=if(status="Closed - True Positive", 1, 0)
    | stats max(label) as is_true_positive by risk_object]
| where isnotnull(is_true_positive)
| fit RandomForestClassifier is_true_positive from total_risk, count into risk_calibration_model
| table risk_object, total_risk, count, predicted(is_true_positive), probability
| where 'predicted(is_true_positive)' = 1
| sort -probability
```

## Visualization

ROC curve (model performance), Table (recalibrated risk objects), Bar chart (feature importance from model).
